outputChannelCompensations.generatedFile: Codex fires PostToolUse for tool calls but the in-payload schema for file-write tools beyond Bash has not been enumerated against the source; F19 routes file-write paths through require-review until per-tool wiring is confirmed.
outputChannelCompensations.commitMessage: Codex commit-text surfaces flow through PostToolUse on git-invoking tools; F19 routes commit-message writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).
outputChannelCompensations.prText: Codex PR-text surfaces flow through PostToolUse on gh-CLI-style tools; F19 routes prText writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).
outputChannelCompensations.finalMessage: Codex does not expose a pre-emit hook for the assistant's final message; F19 routes finalMessage writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).

negativeCapabilities.reason: Codex hook surface does not expose execution-time env baselines; F15 envelope build/verify cannot be wired
negativeCapabilities.evidence: envelopeReporting: false; no env-related fields in the verified PreToolUse / PostToolUse structs (codex-rs/hooks/src/events/pre_tool_use.rs, codex-rs/hooks/src/events/post_tool_use.rs)

negativeCapabilities.reason: MCP tool-handler coverage in codex-rs is incomplete upstream. openai/codex#20204 documents inconsistent PreToolUse hook coverage across tool handlers, and openai/codex#16732 (fixed in openai/codex#18391) showed ApplyPatch was missing from the hook dispatch path entirely. Many MCP-delegated tool handlers may not emit PreToolUse events.
negativeCapabilities.evidence: GitHub issues: openai/codex#20204 (inconsistent hook coverage), openai/codex#16732 (ApplyPatchHandler missing PreToolUse/PostToolUse; fixed in PR #18391)

negativeCapabilities.reason: Whether Codex surfaces Skill invocations through the PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook path has not been verified end-to-end; subject to the same handler-coverage gaps as MCP (openai/codex#20204)
negativeCapabilities.evidence: openai/codex#20204 (inconsistent hook coverage across tool handlers)

negativeCapabilities.reason: No documented pre-emit hook for the assistant final message in Codex; final-message output is neither observed nor intercepted
negativeCapabilities.evidence: outputChannels.finalMessage = none; no pre-emit final-message hook found in codex-rs/hooks/src/events/

negativeCapabilities.reason: PostToolUse + toolOutput are covered via tool_response field in codex-rs/hooks/src/events/post_tool_use.rs; generatedFiles / commitMessage / prText flow through PostToolUse only when their tool handlers emit the event; finalMessage and screenshots have no hook surface
negativeCapabilities.evidence: codex-rs/hooks/src/events/post_tool_use.rs (PostToolUseRequest struct with tool_response field); hook handler coverage gaps per openai/codex#20204

negativeCapabilities.reason: F15 execution-envelope build/verify requires harness-side env baseline capture which Codex does not expose. Decision lattice still re-checks critical writes against contract-declared envelopes; this is the harness limitation, not a Lilara gap.
negativeCapabilities.evidence: envelopeReporting: false; no exec-time envelope surface in codex-rs/hooks/src/

compensatingRestrictions: Decision-engine sources Codex IR trustMeta as: argsFidelity=exact, cwdFidelity=exact, envelopeReporting=false. Floors can rely on the adapter's extractCwd output (i.cwd is an AbsolutePathBuf typed field — codex-rs/hooks/src/types.rs:41) and tool_input.command for the command string.

MCP and Skill tool-handler coverage in Codex is incomplete upstream (openai/codex#20204, #16732). F19 + skill-deny floors apply best-effort and route to require-review until upstream coverage gaps are closed. Operators should not rely on Lilara blocking MCP-delegated tool calls that Codex does not surface through the hook path.

Codex honours exit code 2 for blocks (developers.openai.com/codex/hooks). No harnessOutput:'permission-json' opt-in is needed (contrast with ClawCode, which ignores exit codes and reads stdout JSON). The adapter uses the default harnessOutput:'echo' path.
